product: Column masks now retained when replacing a table
tagline: If a column in the new table matches a column name from the original table, its existing column mask is now retained, even if no mask is specified.
what_it_does: If a column in the new table matches a column name from the original table, its existing column mask is now retained, even if no mask is specified. This change prevents accidental removal of column level security policies during table replacement. Previously, replacing a table dropped all existing column masks, and only newly defined masks were applied.
why_it_matters: ABAC enables you to manage policies using governed tags and apply them consistently across many tables.
plain_summary: If a column in the new table matches a column name from the original table, its existing column mask is now retained, even if no mask is specified. This change prevents accidental removal of column level security policies during table replacement.
key_takeaway: If a column in the new table matches a column name from the original table, its existing column mask is now retained, even if no mask is specified.
